findings.description: User-controlled display_name/action/created_at are interpolated into HTML via f-strings without escaping, producing stored XSS in the admin dashboard.
The title argument is substituted into PAGE_TEMPLATE without HTML escaping, allowing reflected/stored XSS if title is user-influenced.
Docstring promises an inclusive [start, end] range but the query uses created_at < end, so the entire end day is excluded, contradicting the contract.
Comparing created_at against date.isoformat() (date-only) excludes any events whose created_at carries a time component on the end boundary day, reinforcing the off-by-one range bug.
save_report writes to an unvalidated out_path, enabling path traversal or arbitrary file overwrite if out_path is caller/user-influenced.
Row HTML is built via repeated string concatenation (rows += ...) in a loop, which is O(n^2) for large reports; a list plus join is preferable.
The limit parameter is unvalidated; a negative or zero limit in SQLite disables the limit, potentially returning all rows unexpectedly.
event_count duplicates nearly identical SQL across both branches instead of composing the WHERE clause, hurting maintainability.
PAGE_TEMPLATE.format would raise KeyError/IndexError if the template ever gains stray braces, though current substituted values are not re-parsed.
